name: Keralan Fish Curry
tagline: Coconut-tamarind fish curry with curry leaves and mustard tadka
highlight: South Indian coast — coconut and tamarind, no soy needed

hero.alt: Keralan fish curry with coconut gravy and curry leaves
hero.caption: keralan fish — coconut and tamarind

healthFacts: Tamarind provides tartaric acid plus polyphenols — South India's tang of choice.
White fish (kingfish, cod) is high in lean protein and selenium.

steps.title: Tadka the seeds
steps.detail: Heat coconut oil in a heavy pan. Splutter mustard seeds, then add curry leaves — they'll crackle.
steps.tip: Keep a lid handy — mustard seeds jump.

media.alt: Mustard seeds and curry leaves in coconut oil
media.caption: tadka the seeds

steps.title: Soften aromatics
steps.detail: Add onion, ginger, garlic; cook 6 min until soft. Stir in turmeric and chili powder.
steps.tip: Coconut oil stays stable at this heat — perfect for blooming spices.

steps.title: Tamarind and coconut
steps.detail: Whisk tamarind into 1/2 cup water; add to pan. Pour in coconut milk; simmer 5 min.
steps.tip: Don't boil coconut milk hard — it splits.

steps.title: Slip in fish
steps.detail: Slide fish into the gravy. Cover; simmer 6–8 min until fish flakes. Salt to taste. Squeeze of lime.
steps.tip: Don't stir — just shake the pan. Stirring breaks the fish.
